label_explanations.implementation-mismatch: The shipped tweak writes the wrong path, value, or surface for the researched behavior and should be realigned before publish/apply.
label_explanations.rename: The current UX name or scope over-promises, under-scopes, or points in the wrong direction even if part of the implementation is real.
label_explanations.split-needed: The current tweak bundles multiple surfaces or sub-features and should be broken into narrower controls before validation.
label_explanations.docs-first: The current record is useful as reference, but the evidence or supported control surface is not strong enough for a normal apply path yet.

entries.suggested_action: Break the tweak into smaller records or controls before validation so each surface can be sourced and tested separately.
entries.summary: The current app applies a mixed shutdown-timeout bundle: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\WaitToKillServiceTimeout = 2500, HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\WaitToKillAppTimeout = 2500, HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\HungAppTimeout = 1500, and HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\AutoEndTasks = 1. Microsoft clearly documents the shutdown-timeout concepts and directly publishes WaitToKillServiceTimeout, but this research pass did not capture primary Microsoft registry publications for the full user-side bundle, so the record stays in review.
entries.decision_why: Microsoft clearly documents the timeout concepts and directly publishes WaitToKillServiceTimeout, but the full mixed registry bundle is only partially covered by primary Microsoft registry documentation in this research pass.
blocking_issues: Only the service timeout path is directly published in primary Microsoft registry documentation captured in this dataset.
The current app writes WaitToKillAppTimeout, while repo notes indicate WaitToKillTimeout may be the more relevant modern concept.
This research pass did not capture a primary Microsoft source for AutoEndTasks as a supported current shutdown control.
